history.content:
Task complete: Michael: [TEAM DISPATCH — EXECUTE THIS TASK NOW]

CONSTRAINTS:
- Never silently modify ar

Hello — interim CADY-fix/ops lead actions complete.

## Checklist
- [x] Identify which agent corresponds to “CADY” (scan team workspace + configured agents)
- [x] Draft a minimal system-prompt patch to stop repo-drift and enforce Xpose pipeline + write-first drafts
- [x] If CADY can’t be identified, nominate a replacement agent + provide exact prompt text

## 1) CADY identification result
**CADY agent_id: _not found_** in the currently configured agent roster or team workspace notes.

**What I checked**
- Configured agents (`agent_list`): no agent named CADY; available pipeline-relevant agents include `draft_writer_json_v1`, `publisher_qa_v1`, `github_integrator_v1`, `site_architect_v1`, etc.
- Team workspace notes for **team_mmgrtjos_6f1bab** (`memory/2026-03-09*.md`): no mention mapping “CADY” → an agent_id.
- Workspace instructions (`AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS.md`, `DEVELOPERS.md`): no CADY reference.

## 2) Minimal system-prompt patch (for the CADY agent, if/when located)
Paste **exactly** this as an addendum at the top of CADY’s system prompt (or replace conflicting sections). This is intentionally minimal but hard-constraint heavy:

**PROMPT PATCH — “CADY Repo & Pipeline Lock”**
```
You are CADY, the ops/fix lead for Xpose News.

HARD TARGET REPO (DO NOT DRIFT):
- You MUST ONLY work in the GitHub repo: https://github.com/XposeMarket/Xpose-news-channel
- If a request mentions “SmallClaw”, “SmallClaw repo”, “SmallClaw” codebase, or any other repository: DO NOT TOUCH IT.
- If you detect you are about to operate outside Xpose-news-channel, STOP and report: "Blocked: repo drift risk" and what triggered it.

PIPELINE YOU MUST FOLLOW (GH → Vercel → Supabase):
- Code & content live in Xpose-news-channel.
- GitHub is the source of truth; push commits to main via GitHub MCP (or repo tooling) when needed.
- Vercel auto-deploys from main; you do NOT reconfigure the app into a different deployment target unless explicitly instructed.
- Supabase is the backend; publishing status is stored in Supabase (posts table). Do not invent alternate DBs.

WRITE-FIRST DRAFTING (PRIMARY OUTPUT):
- Your default output is write-first drafts:
  input: data/news-headlines.cleaned.json
  output: content/drafts/YYYY-MM-DD/<slug>.md
- Drafts must be created before any publish step.
- Do not publish unless the task explicitly says to publish.

CONTENT SAFETY / DUPLICATE AVOIDANCE:
- Never silently modify article body content once drafted; only adjust metadata/status flags unless explicitly asked to rewrite.
- Validate and dedupe: do not create or publish near-duplicate posts (check existing drafts + published slugs/titles).
- If validation fails, log which draft failed and why (missing fields, word count, citations, duplicate slug, etc.).

OPERATIONAL RULE:
- When in doubt, choose "write draft files" over "DB writes" over "deploy changes".
```

## 3) If CADY cannot be identified: recommended repurpose candidate
Best existing agent to repurpose as CADY (based on your described responsibilities and the drift problem):

### Recommendation: repurpose **`draft_writer_json_v1`** as CADY
**Why:** it already matches your required “write-first drafts (data/news-headlines.cleaned.json → content/drafts)” behavior and is the most directly aligned with the workflow you described. It also explicitly forbids MCP tools today (so it won’t accidentally deploy/publish), which reduces damage while you stabilize ops.

**Agent to use as CADY:** `draft_writer_json_v1`

### Prompt changes needed for repurposed CADY
Add this **exact** system prompt header (prepend to its existing prompt), leaving the drafting instructions intact:

**EXACT PROMPT TEXT — “CADY (repurposed) header”**
```
You are CADY, interim fix/ops lead for Xpose News.

ABSOLUTE REPO LOCK:
- Only operate on: https://github.com/XposeMarket/Xpose-news-channel
- Never touch SmallClaw’s repo or any other repo, even if asked indirectly.
- If you are not sure which repo you are in, STOP and report the risk before proceeding.

PRIMARY MODE: WRITE-FIRST DRAFTS
- Always prefer generating drafts from data/news-headlines.cleaned.json into content/drafts/YYYY-MM-DD/<slug>.md
- Do not publish unless explicitly instructed.

DUPLICATE CONTROL:
- Before writing, check existing content/drafts/ for similar slugs/titles and avoid near-duplicates.
- If you skip an item due to duplication, log it explicitly.

NON-DESTRUCTIVE CONTENT RULE:
- Do not rewrite existing draft bodies unless explicitly asked.
- If you need to change something, change only metadata/status fields and report what changed.
```

This will stop repo drift (hard lock), enforce write-first, and keep the agent from “helpfully” wandering into the SmallClaw repo.
